name: NanoTS
description: NanoTS is a lightweight, high-performance embedded time-series database optimized for real-time streaming applications like video, finance, and IoT sensor data. It uses memory-mapped storage with lock-free data structures for lightning-fast writes (8.83us on SSD) and sub-microsecond reads. Supports pre-allocated or growable storage, multiple independent streams in a single file, bidirectional iteration with timestamp seeking, and crash recovery with frame-level atomicity. Like SQLite, it runs in-process with no server required.
format_on_disk: Memory-Mapped Binary Blocks (single .nts file + SQLite metadata)
core_strengths: 113,000+ writes/second sustained on SSD with lock-free storage
Pre-allocated storage eliminates disk-full surprises and fragmentation
Multiple independent streams in a single file with separate write contexts
Configurable block sizes for durability/performance trade-offs
Auto-reclaim mode recycles oldest blocks when space runs out
Composite (timestamp, secondary_key) ordering for non-unique timestamps
Crash recovery with frame-level atomicity guarantees
best_use_cases: Video surveillance: capture dozens of camera streams concurrently
IoT sensor data: high-frequency readings with time-based queries
Financial tick data: microsecond-precision trade storage and replay
Embedded robotics: lidar, IMU, GPS streams with time-aligned scrubbing
Scientific data acquisition: multi-channel time-series at line rate
remarks: NanoTS uses a hybrid architecture combining SQLite for stream metadata with memory-mapped binary block files for frame data. Each stream supports exactly one writer, but unlimited concurrent readers. Frames are write-once (immutable after writing). The current on-disk format is v2, which is not backwards-compatible with v1 — migrate by re-writing data into a freshly-allocated v2 file. Pre-allocated mode is recommended for embedded/fixed-budget deployments; growable mode is available for general workloads.
